MiCA PKA Device API (gxio/pka.h)
[gxio Low-Level IO Device Control API]

Detailed Description

The Public Key Authentication acceleration hardware module provides public key cryptography operations at three levels of complexity:.

1. Basic long vector mathematical operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide, shift, compare, ....),

2. Modular exponentiation (with and without using the Chinese Remainder Theorem), Modular inversion, Elliptic Curve point addition/doubling/multiplication,

3. High-level protocol operations like ECDSA sign/verify, DSA sign/verify

These operations are submitted to the hardware by placing a command descriptor and operands onto a command ring. The hardware completes the operation and puts the result onto a result ring. The number of command/result rings (1 - 4), the size of the elements in each ring, and whether one ring has priority over the others is configurable at initialization time. The command and result descriptors, and functions to configure, manage and access the rings, are described below.

Data Structures

struct  pka_vector_t
 A data vector. More...
struct  pka_cmd_desc_t
 A PKA command descriptor. More...
struct  pka_res_desc_t
 The result descriptor. More...
struct  gxio_mica_pka_context_t
 A context object used to manage MiCA PKA hardware resources. More...
struct  gxio_mica_pka_trng_context_t
 A context object used to manage MiCA PKA TRNG hardware resources. More...
struct  gxio_mica_pka_ring_config_t
 Describes how to configure a PKA engine's command result rings. More...

Defines

#define PKA_NUM_RINGS   4
 The maximum number of command/result rings supported by the hardware.

Functions

int gxio_mica_pka_init (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, int mica_index, gxio_mica_pka_ring_config_t *ring_config)
 Initialize context, and initialize PKA engine's rings.
int gxio_mica_pka_command_slots_available (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, int ring)
 Determine if a PKA ring has room for a new command.
int gxio_mica_pka_put_command_on_ring (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, int ring, pka_cmd_desc_t *cmd)
 Put a command on a ring to be executed.
int gxio_mica_pka_results_available (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, int ring)
 Determine if a result is available on a PKA ring.
int gxio_mica_pka_get_result_from_ring (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, int ring, pka_res_desc_t *res)
 Get a PKA result from a ring.
int gxio_mica_pka_cfg_cmd_queue_empty_interrupt (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, int target_cpu, int target_event, int ring)
 Configure an interrupt to fire when the command queue is empty.
int gxio_mica_pka_cfg_res_queue_full_interrupt (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, int target_cpu, int target_event, int ring)
 Configure an interrupt to fire when the result ring has results.
void gxio_mica_pka_read_interrupts (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, uint32_t *interrupts)
 Read the PKA interrupt status bits.
void gxio_mica_pka_reset_interrupts (gxio_mica_pka_context_t *context, uint32_t interrupts)
 Reset the selected PKA interrupts.

Define Documentation

#define PKA_NUM_RINGS   4

The maximum number of command/result rings supported by the hardware.

Function Documentation

int gxio_mica_pka_init ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
int  mica_index,
gxio_mica_pka_ring_config_t *  ring_config 
)

Initialize context, and initialize PKA engine's rings.

Parameters:
context Context object to be initialized.
mica_index The index of the MiCA shim with which this context will be associated.
ring_config The parameters describing how to initialize the PKA engine rings.
Returns:
0 on success, negative error code on failure.

int gxio_mica_pka_command_slots_available ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
int  ring 
)

Determine if a PKA ring has room for a new command.

Parameters:
context An initialized PKA context.
ring The ring number in the context to query.
Returns:
1 if there is room on the command ring for a new command, 0 if not.

int gxio_mica_pka_put_command_on_ring ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
int  ring,
pka_cmd_desc_t *  cmd 
)

Put a command on a ring to be executed.

Parameters:
context An initialized PKA context.
ring The number of ring in the context onto which to put the command.
cmd The command to queue for execution.
Returns:
0 on success, negative error code on failure.

int gxio_mica_pka_results_available ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
int  ring 
)

Determine if a result is available on a PKA ring.

Parameters:
context An initialized PKA context.
ring The ring number in the context to query.
Returns:
1 if a result is available, 0 if not.

int gxio_mica_pka_get_result_from_ring ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
int  ring,
pka_res_desc_t *  res 
)

Get a PKA result from a ring.

Parameters:
context An initialized PKA context.
ring The ring number in the context from which to get the result.
res Pointer to where to put the result.
Returns:
0 on success, negative error code on failure.

int gxio_mica_pka_cfg_cmd_queue_empty_interrupt ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
int  target_cpu,
int  target_event,
int  ring 
)

Configure an interrupt to fire when the command queue is empty.

Parameters:
context An initialized PKA context.
target_cpu CPU number of interrupt target tile.
target_event Specific event which will be set in the target IPI register when the interrupt occurs.
ring The ring in the context to monitor.
Returns:
0 on success, negative error code on failure.

int gxio_mica_pka_cfg_res_queue_full_interrupt ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
int  target_cpu,
int  target_event,
int  ring 
)

Configure an interrupt to fire when the result ring has results.

Parameters:
context An initialized PKA context.
target_cpu CPU number of interrupt target tile.
target_event Specific event which will be set in the target IPI register when the interrupt occurs.
ring The ring in the context to monitor.
Returns:
0 on success, negative error code on failure.

void gxio_mica_pka_read_interrupts ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
uint32_t *  interrupts 
)

Read the PKA interrupt status bits.

Parameters:
context An initialized PKA context.
interrupts Pointer to the result interrupt status. A '1' in a bit position resets the corresponding interrupt. The low-order 4 bits are the command ring empty interrupts, bits 5-7 are the result ring full interrupts.

void gxio_mica_pka_reset_interrupts ( gxio_mica_pka_context_t *  context,
uint32_t  interrupts 
)

Reset the selected PKA interrupts.

Parameters:
context An initialized PKA context.
interrupts A '1' in the bit position resets the corresponding interrupt. The low-order 4 bits are the command ring empty interrupts, bits 5-7 are the result ring full interrupts.
